creator: yuri@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Robert Clausecker from comment #17)



Hi Robert,


Why is bundling OpenSSL a particularly huge security risk?

There are hundreds of Rust-based ports that bundle OpenSSL, usually from the same Rust crates. IMO this shouldn't stop port updates from being committed, otherwise many updates (like this one) will only make it in months or years.

That's how Rust works in general - it routinely bundles numerous software packages. Maybe Rust itself should be deemed a security risk?


Yuri
